Nicotine.mechanism: nAChR activation -> dopamine release -> I decrease
Nicotine.bbb_note: Lipophilic amine, rapid BBB penetration (7-10s to brain)
Nicotine.ref: Benowitz 2009 Clin Pharmacol Ther; Dani & De Biasi 2001

THC.mechanism: CB1 agonism -> sanggeuk (overcoming) cycle suppression -> I decrease
THC.ref: Mechoulam & Parker 2013 Annu Rev Psychol; Zou & Kumar 2018

SSRI.bbb_note: Designed for BBB penetration (variable by compound)
SSRI.ref: Stahl 2013 Essential Psychopharmacology; Hieronymus et al. 2016

Psilocybin.ref: Carhart-Harris et al. 2012 PNAS; Nichols 2016 Pharmacol Rev

LSD.mechanism: 5-HT2A partial agonism -> DMN suppression -> I decrease
LSD.ref: Preller et al. 2018 Curr Biol; Nichols 2016 Pharmacol Rev

DMT.ref: Strassman 2001 DMT: The Spirit Molecule; Barker 2018 Front Neurosci

Ketamine.mechanism: NMDA antagonism -> glutamate surge -> rapid I decrease
Ketamine.ref: Berman et al. 2000 Biol Psychiatry; Zanos & Gould 2018 Pharmacol Rev

TTX.mechanism: Nav1.x sodium channel blockade -> excitation suppression -> I increase
TTX.bbb_note: Hydrophilic guanidinium toxin; BBB penetration poorly characterized. Peripheral effects dominate. Central effects require direct injection or compromised BBB. Dolphin exposure is sublingual/mucosal.
TTX.ref: Narahashi 2008 Proc Jpn Acad Ser B; Lago et al. 2015 Mar Drugs

Muscimol.mechanism: GABA-A direct agonism -> strong inhibition -> I increase
Muscimol.bbb_note: Small molecule, crosses BBB (Amanita muscaria active metabolite)
Muscimol.ref: Johnston 2014 Neurochem Res; Michelot & Melendez-Howell 2003 Mycol Res

('QCD Resonance Ladder', 'Higgs Decay bb.reason: FULLY INDEPENDENT. Finding 1 uses QCD meson masses (rho, J/psi, Upsilon) — bound states of light/charm/bottom quarks. Finding 2 uses Higgs boson branching ratios (electroweak decay widths). Different particles, different physics sector (QCD vs electroweak), different observables (masses vs decay fractions). No shared data whatsoever.

('QCD Resonance Ladder', 'Quark-Lepton B.reason: INDEPENDENT. Finding 1 uses meson masses (rho, J/psi, Upsilon) which are quark-antiquark bound states. Finding 3 uses bare quark masses (charm, up) and lepton mass (muon). Although J/psi contains charm quarks, the J/psi MASS (~3097 MeV) is a bound-state observable very different from the bare charm quark mass (~1275 MeV). Different observables, different physics (bound-state spectroscopy vs fundamental mass relations).

('QCD Resonance Ladder', 'Achromatic Exc.overlap: rho, J/psi, Upsilon are among the 84 particles in the all-pairs scan
('QCD Resonance Ladder', 'Achromatic Exc.reason: PARTIAL OVERLAP. The 3 mesons (rho, J/psi, Upsilon) from Finding 1 are included in the 84 particles scanned in Finding 4. However, Finding 1 tests a SPECIFIC hypothesis (integer ratio ladder) while Finding 4 tests a DIFFERENT hypothesis (excess of ratio~6 pairs). The overlap is in input data but not in the tested pattern. Conservatively flagged as dependent.

('Higgs Decay bb+tautau', 'Quark-Lepton.reason: FULLY INDEPENDENT. Finding 2 uses Higgs branching ratios (decay widths to bb and tautau). Finding 3 uses fundamental fermion masses (charm, up, muon). Although both reference fermions, Finding 2 measures DECAY RATES (proportional to coupling^2 * mass^2 * phase space) while Finding 3 measures MASSES. Completely different observables. No particle overlap (b,tau vs charm,up,muon).

('Higgs Decay bb+tautau', 'Achromatic Ex.overlap: b-quark and tau are among the 84 particles
('Higgs Decay bb+tautau', 'Achromatic Ex.reason: PARTIAL OVERLAP. The b-quark and tau-lepton appear in both findings. Finding 2 tests their Higgs coupling strengths (branching ratios), while Finding 4 tests mass ratio statistics. Different observables but shared particles. Conservatively flagged as dependent.

('Quark-Lepton Bridge', 'Achromatic Exce.overlap: charm, up, muon are among the 84 particles in the all-pairs scan
('Quark-Lepton Bridge', 'Achromatic Exce.reason: OVERLAP. charm, up, and muon masses are used in Finding 3 and are also among the 84 particles in Finding 4's all-pairs scan. Both findings examine mass relationships, making them potentially correlated. Conservatively treated as DEPENDENT.

central_charge.note: central charge counts degrees of freedom

yukawa_couplings.parameter: 13 Yukawa coupling constants (6 quark masses, 3 lepton masses, 4 CKM)
yukawa_couplings.status: Partially addressed — TECS-L gives mass formulas but not from first principles

pmns_matrix.parameter: 7 PMNS parameters (3 angles, 1 CP phase, 3 neutrino masses)

strong_cp.status: Not derived — why is theta ~ 0? Axion needed?

cosmological_constant.status: Not derived — the worst fine-tuning problem in physics

dark_matter.parameter: Dark matter particle identity and mass

dark_energy.parameter: Dark energy equation of state w

quantum_gravity.status: R(n)=1 gives graviton dof but not full quantum gravity

baryon_asymmetry.status: Interestingly eta ~ 6e-10 contains P1=6, but not derived

0: Maximize exposure diversity -- everything is novel
Learning rate is high; expect rapid weight shifts
1: Introduce repeating patterns to build recognition
Dream engine should be active (intensity=0.5) for consolidation
2: Habituation active -- vary inputs to sustain curiosity
3: Focus on depth over breadth -- curiosity is selective
Mitosis threshold low -- expect active specialization
4: Stable self achieved -- slow learning protects identity
Metacognition depth 3 -- deep self-referential loops active
